title: Process Capability Row
description: One manufacturing process's spatial / kinematic / energetic / tolerance envelope at a point in time. The per-process half of the Manufacturing Capability Index (claude-skills#205). Consumed by an AI design agent before it commits geometry, and the empirical substrate Koop's Law (#204) fits its curve against.

min_feature_mm.description: Spatial floor: smallest reliably producible feature (e.g. min bit radius * 2).

max_envelope_mm.description: Spatial ceiling: max work/build volume in mm.

kinematic_cadence.description: Speed before deflection/warp. Aspirational under load; not gateable today (see README).

energetic_threshold_j_per_cm3.description: Energy to alter/remove a unit volume in this process's dominant state of matter.

tolerance_floor_mm.description: Sigma-capability: tightest tolerance the process holds (+/- mm).

states_of_matter.description: States the process manipulates; gates the states-of-matter / thermodynamics challenge modifiers.

as_of.description: Capability-timeline snapshot, e.g. '2026-Q3'. Rows are time-stamped because the index is a moving boundary.

gateable_today.description: True if a MakerBench seed can enforce this row's spatial/tolerance fields against a real grader today.

source.description: Provenance: vendor spec, measured run, or estimate.
